data.purpose: Large live readouts — token totals, the headline number on an insight card. Tabular, weight 700.

title.purpose: Section and card headings, panel names.

body.purpose: Rows and primary text. The 11px base size of the instrument.

label.purpose: Uppercase meta, badges, and the small tags on controls. The dense cockpit's connective tissue.

mono.purpose: Geist Mono for ids, paths, code, diffs, and aligned numeric columns only — never decorative.

shadows.name: menu
shadows.purpose: Dropdown and context menus raised off the canvas.

shadows.name: popover
shadows.purpose: Tooltips and detached floating panels — the highest everyday layer.

shadows.name: modal
shadows.purpose: Confirmation dialogs; the rare full-attention layer.

shadows.name: inset-ring
shadows.purpose: 1px keyboard-focus keyline on chrome where an outline would clip.

motion.name: meter-fill
motion.purpose: Meter and time-bar fills — calm ballistics, not twitchy.

motion.name: reveal
motion.purpose: The active-tab underline wipe; the system's one expressive easing.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Crossfade or instant; meter fills snap to value, the underline appears without a wipe.

breakpoints.name: hud-min
breakpoints.purpose: Default always-on-top window width; the PFD must stay legible at this size.

components.name: Usage Meter Row
components.description: The PFD hero: label/value/countdown over a thin track with a discrete severity fill and a pace marker. The clearest expression of the Glass Cockpit.
components.html: Sonnet · 5h68%2h 14m

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one place blue becomes a background — a committed/active action. Used sparingly.

components.name: Feature Tab
components.description: The cockpit's primary navigation gesture: a ghost text tab whose active state grows a wiping underline.

components.name: Status Toggle
components.description: Provider/feature switch whose tone IS the status light: on=green, off=dim, setup=amber, unavailable=red, busy=blue (pulsing).

components.name: Range Tabs
components.description: Segmented time-range selector on a recessed ground; the active segment fills.

components.name: Provider Badge
components.description: The fixed provider color-code as a pill: Claude blue, Codex cyan, MiniMax violet, Agent orchid. One provider, one color, every surface.

components.name: Search Input
components.description: Text field with a signal-blue focus ring (the corrected behavior — green focus is forbidden, it collides with the meter).

components.name: Insight Card
components.description: Resting surface defined by a graphite fill + 1px border, no shadow. Big tabular value, dim label, severity-tinted trend.

components.name: Tooltip
components.description: Square dark popover, 1px border, popover shadow. No arrow, no diamond — the instrument doesn't decorate.
components.html: Working-context preservation keeps large transient data out of the transcript.

components.name: Analytics Tab
components.description: Underline-indicator tab (never a pill). The 2px bottom border takes the tab's own accent when active.

narrative.overview: Quill is an instrument, not a dashboard. A glass cockpit is a calm, dark panel that surfaces system state and tiers its alarms by severity, so the operator stays ahead of the aircraft without staring. Quill watches what a coding agent can't see about itself — what it is burning and where it stands against the limits — and reports it with the exactness of a flight instrument. The aesthetic borrows the principles of that world (a strict color budget, severity tiers, numbers that never jitter, hairlines instead of chrome), not its skin. The system runs in two densities of the same instrument: the always-on-top Primary Flight Display (the dense live analytics you monitor at a glance) and the roomier Systems Pages (search, learning, plugins, agents, memory, settings — the same instrument zoomed out, for managing). Same tokens, same control vocabulary, inverted spacing.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black canvas, chosen for contrast headroom.
A reserved three-color severity meter (green / amber / red) is the system's spine.
Color is semantic-only and on a strict budget; chrome is grayscale.
Numbers are tabular and never reflow; values are bright, labels are dim.
Structure is carried by 1px hairlines and flat tonal panels, never by cards and shadows.
Two densities, one identity: a dense cockpit and roomy systems pages.

rules.name: The Severity Code Rule
rules.body: Green, amber, and red are reserved for threshold state. They never decorate, brand, or indicate category. If a green thing is not 'healthy', it is a bug.

rules.name: The Reserved-Status Rule
rules.body: Provider identity (cyan / violet / orchid) is drawn from a cool set that never overlaps the severity meter. A provider hue rendering as green or amber is forbidden — the exact drift this system was built to kill.

rules.name: The Dimming Ladder Rule
rules.body: Hierarchy is built by brightness, not hue. Step down a ladder of white alpha or Readout to Label to Label-Faint. Reach for a new color only when it carries new meaning.

rules.name: The Tabular Rule
rules.body: Every live or comparative number uses tabular figures. A readout that reflows as its value ticks is broken.

rules.name: The Mono-for-Truth Rule
rules.body: Monospace is for things that are code or identifiers. It is never used on a label to look technical; jitter-free digits are tabular-nums, not a mono font.

rules.name: The Floats-Only Rule
rules.body: A shadow means 'this floats'. A surface sitting on the canvas never has one. Glow, neon, and colored shadows are forbidden.

dos: Do reserve green / amber / red for status only, on the 50% / 80% thresholds.
Do give every provider exactly one fixed color from the cool identity ramp, never a severity hue.
Do set font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums on every live or compared number.
Do structure with 1px hairlines and flat graphite panels.
Do make values bright and labels dim; build hierarchy with brightness and weight, not new hues.
Do invert density by mode — pack the PFD, give the Systems Pages room — keeping one control vocabulary.
Do keep motion functional and fast, and honor prefers-reduced-motion.
donts: Don't ship a generic SaaS template: no gradient hero, no big-number metric panels, no pill buttons, no identical card grids.
Don't reach for AI-hype / crypto finishes: no neon gradients, no glassmorphism, no glow, no colored shadows.
Don't go playful-consumer: no bubbly palette, no radius above 8px (pills excepted), no emoji, no mascots.
Don't drift corporate-enterprise: no stock-photo blue, no gray-on-gray with acres of padding and three numbers on screen.
Don't let a provider change color between panels, and don't let a category hue borrow a status color.
Don't focus inputs in green; focus is signal-blue.
Don't use proportional figures for live numbers.
Don't cram editable controls into the always-on PFD; they belong in the Systems Pages.
Don't add another launcher window to the titlebar junk drawer; consolidate, don't extend.
